title: Siya, Chetan 'rehearsed' Ketan's murder on another hill before executing it at Lohagad Fort
content: In a shocking turn of events, Pune Rural police revealed that realtor Ketan Agarwal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly rehearsed his murder before pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort. The couple confessed to practicing the act on a hill near Market Yard. Police are planning a scene reconstruction and gait analysis of Chaudhary.

title: Russia turns to India for gasoline as fuel shortages deepen after Ukrainian strikes
content: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that it was in discussions with several countries about importing fuel at acceptable prices. One industry source said India has already shipped at least 60,000 metric tonnes of gasoline to Russia. Another source said two cargoes, each carrying between 30,000 and 40,000 tonnes, have been dispatched.

title: Bengaluru daycare horror: Toddlers put in washing machine, sprayed with toilet jet
content: Five women daycare staff at an IT company campus in Bengaluru face an FIR for allegedly abusing toddlers. Disturbing videos surfaced, showing caregivers threatening and physically mistreating children aged two to three. Allegations include locking children in bathrooms and using a washing machine. Police are questioning the accused and verifying the footage to determine the extent of the abuse.

title: 'Carrying water is not degrading': Ashwin addresses criticism over Sooryavanshi
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has defended his stance on young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's role, emphasizing that carrying drinks and observing from the bench offers valuable learning.  He clarified his earlier comments, stating that such tasks are not demeaning and contribute to a player's understanding of team dynamics.  Despite a stellar IPL, Sooryavanshi's debut is delayed, with Ashwin suggesting patience for the 15-year-old's promising career.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: Stock market: Which are top gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian stock markets surged on Wednesday, with the Sensex and Nifty reclaiming key levels after a two-day slump. Positive global cues, including falling crude oil prices, boosted investor confidence. Sectors like banking and consumer goods saw significant gains, while IT stocks faced pressure. Analysts anticipate a positive second half of the year, citing easing geopolitical tensions and potential trade agreements.

title: Army chief Dhiraj Seth unveils 'VIJAY' vision for future-ready Indian Army
content: General Seth, who took over as the 31st Chief of the Army Staff on Tuesday after General Upendra Dwivedi retired on June 30, addressed reporters after receiving a guard of honour at South Block. Speaking on his first day as Army chief, he described the Indian Army as a force that is fully prepared for combat. He said taking over the Army's top post was both an honour and a responsibility.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: How Integrated Battle Groups could change the way Indian Army fights future wars
content: The Indian Army has launched a major structural overhaul, creating five Integrated Battle Groups and a Fire Support Group. This move aims to enhance rapid, decisive military responses by integrating infantry, armour, artillery, and logistics under single commanders. The reform, tested under the 17 Mountain Strike Corps, streamlines mobilisation and empowers field commanders for quicker action, building a more agile and lethal future-ready force.

title: Centre issues show cause notice to Delhi Gymkhana Club in eviction proceedings
content: According to the notice, the Club's continued occupation of the land after the government resumed possession for a public purpose amounts to "unauthorized occupation" under Section 2(g) of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorized Occupants) Act, 1971. The government has maintained that the President of India exercised powers under Clause 4 of the perpetual lease deed, which allows the lessor to resume the property if it is required for a public purpose.

title: Bata billed customer Rs 67 over MRP, told to refund and pay compensation
content: Bata India has been held liable by the Kerala consumer commission for overcharging a student Rs 67 above the printed MRP on a pair of shoes. The commission ruled that retailers cannot legally charge more than the MRP unless specific procedures, like clearly marking revised prices on the product and publishing in newspapers, are followed.

title: Palantir, Nvidia building AI for US that has other nations fearing a 'kill switch'
content: Palantir and Nvidia have built an air-gapped AI system that lets US agencies run frontier-grade models on American chips, keeping the weights and data fully in Washington's control. Days later, Palantir's "AI sovereignty" manifesto warned that depending on someone else's AI means surrendering your future, a pitch that reads very differently for nations now weighing whether to run on a US-designed stack.

title: Do France have the most terrific World Cup attack of all time?
content: On Day 20 of the World Cup, France continued to march in the World Cup like Napolean through Europe. Norway and Halaand are also in the picture while Mexico finally won its 5th game in a World Cup. Read this and more in this edition of Offside.

title: Hardik Pandya trade: CSK enter the race; seven franchises express interest
content: Seven IPL franchises have expressed interest in signing Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder decided to leave Mumbai Indians. Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have joined Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Rajasthan Royals in the race, with talks gathering pace. Player consent, leadership opportunities and trade structures are expected to shape one of the biggest IPL moves in recent years.

title: Abhishek Sharma's year-long reign as World No. 1 ends in latest ICC T20I rankings
content: Ishan Kishan has claimed the top spot in the T20I batting rankings, edging out his teammate Abhishek Sharma with a remarkable performance. This is a notable achievement for Kishan, whose recent form, especially in the T20 World Cup, has been impressive. He now joins the ranks of elite Indian batsmen. Both players are preparing for the upcoming England series.

title: Ram Temple Trust chairman admitted to ICU with urinary tract infection
content: Mahant Nritya Gopal Das, chairman of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, has been admitted to the ICU in Lucknow after being diagnosed with a urinary tract infection. The 89-year-old was found unconscious and is under critical care. This development comes just days before a crucial trust meeting on July 6, which will address the resignation of two trustees amid controversy over missing donations.

title: 13-year-old parrot battles rare egg-binding; family seeks treatment in Delhi
content: A 13-year-old parrot named Mithu faces a life-threatening condition as a large, calcified egg is stuck in its pelvic region, preventing natural laying.  Unable to find specialized avian surgery in Kolkata, Mithu's family is taking the bird to Delhi for an expensive operation.  Veterinarians are exploring affordable options to save the beloved pet, highlighting the challenges of reproductive disorders in caged birds.

title: No brain, heart, lungs: Indian sailor dies in Venezuela, kin says organs missing
content: A shocking discovery has emerged regarding the death of Indian seafarer Rakesh Chauhan in Venezuela. His body, returned to India without vital organs and with extensive prior stitching, has raised grave suspicions. The seafarers' union is demanding a thorough investigation into the alleged foul play and a cover-up, urging immediate intervention from the Indian Embassy for justice and compensation for the grieving family.

title: Hormuz oil shock sends India back to Russia: Is this a peak or the new normal?
content: Russia has emerged as the dominant supplier of crude oil for India - with imports touching over 240 million barrels in the four months since the US-Iran conflict began. In fact, crude imports from Russia are over 4 times the nearest supplier - UAE at over 58 million barrels.

title: Why Navies Sink Old Ships: US tests weapons on ex-USS Juneau; India runs similar drills
content: The US Navy recently sank the decommissioned USS Juneau in a "SinkEx" exercise off the Mariana Islands. This drill, involving air, surface, and submarine forces, tested weapon systems and provided crucial real-world training. Such exercises, also conducted by the Indian Navy, validate ordnance effectiveness and demonstrate operational readiness. Rear Admiral Eric Anduze highlighted the integration of joint capabilities for high-end Pacific operations.

title: Déjà vu! Kluivert repeats father Patrick's penalty heartbreak 26 yrs later - Watch
content: Football witnessed a poignant echo of the past as Justin Kluivert, like his father Patrick 26 years prior, missed a crucial penalty, contributing to the Netherlands' shock FIFA World Cup 2026 exit against Morocco. The Dutch suffered their earliest ever World Cup elimination after a dramatic 3-2 penalty shootout loss. Morocco's heroics, including a late equaliser and a stellar goalkeeping performance, secured their place in the next round.

title: The new family member has fur: India’s pet parenting boom goes mainstream
content: Pets in urban India are no longer just companions; they've become integral family members, akin to children. This profound shift is evident in the burgeoning pet-care industry, offering everything from birthday parties to specialized healthcare.  This evolution reflects changing social dynamics, including nuclear families and increased urban loneliness, where pets provide vital emotional support and routine, fostering deep, reciprocal bonds.

title: Watch: Australian goes to Thailand hotel with minor, comes out with body in suitcase
content: An Australian man, Simon Peter Carman, has been charged with the murder of a 17-year-old Thai girl in Pattaya. Her body was discovered in a suitcase near a railway line. CCTV footage reportedly shows Carman leaving a condominium with the suitcase before abandoning it. He was arrested attempting to flee the country and denies all charges, claiming the girl became 'crazy' and threatened him.

title: 'Portrayed as Lord Krishna': BJP slams SP for 'mocking Hindus' over Akhilesh's birthday havan
content: The BJP has strongly criticized the Samajwadi Party, accusing them of disrespecting Hindus after workers depicted Akhilesh Yadav as Lord Krishna during a birthday ritual. This accusation comes amid controversy over alleged Ram Temple donation irregularities. Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath also attacked Yadav, urging him to visit the Ram Temple and recalling past SP actions against devotees.

title: AP EAPCET result declared: Check direct link to download rank card
content: JNTU Kakinada has released the AP EAPCET 2026 results today, July 1. Over 1.82 lakh students qualified in Engineering, with girls achieving a higher pass rate.  More than 63,000 candidates secured ranks in Agriculture and Pharmacy.  Rank cards are available for download on the official website, paving the way for upcoming counselling sessions for admissions across Andhra Pradesh.

title: Cops recreate crime scene, gait analysis of Chetan; dummy used matching Ketan’s weight
content: Pune Rural Police recreated a murder scene at Lohagad Fort, bringing accused Chetan Chaudhary for forensic gait analysis to match CCTV footage. This step aims to verify his involvement in the death of Ketan Agrawal, who fell from the fort on June 18. Investigators suspect a conspiracy involving Agrawal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and Chaudhary. A fast-track court has been approved for a speedy trial.

title: Big relief for Sreesanth! Kerala CA revokes three-year ban after apology
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth's three-year ban by the Kerala Cricket Association has been revoked. The decision follows Sreesanth's unconditional apology for his critical remarks against the association. This lifts the suspension, allowing him to continue as co-owner of the Aries Kollam Sailors franchise in the Kerala Cricket League. The KCA warned of strict action for any future misconduct.

title: 'HC went beyond ... ': Vijay-led TN govt moves SC challenging cow slaughter ban even on Bakrid
content: Tamil Nadu's government has approached the Supreme Court, challenging the Madras high court's order for a complete ban on cow slaughter. The state argues the high court exceeded its jurisdiction by imposing a blanket prohibition, even on authorized slaughterhouses. Existing laws, they contend, regulate rather than prohibit the slaughter of older, unfit cows, and the government seeks an interim stay on the high court's directive.

title: Odisha’s JAGA Mission: How slum dwellers get land rights, not just houses
content: Odisha's JAGA Mission is revolutionizing urban development by granting slum dwellers legal land rights, moving beyond mere housing. Launched in 2017, the initiative transforms existing slums into liveable neighbourhoods through infrastructure upgrades and community participation. This approach secures tenure, boosts confidence for home improvements, and integrates these communities into the city's fabric, fostering a more inclusive urban future.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' BO collection: Movie eyes Rs 90-100 crore week 1 finish
content: Ahmed Khan's 'Welcome to the Jungle', starring Akshay Kumar, Suniel Shetty and Paresh Rawal, is inching toward the Rs 90 crore domestic net mark as it enters Day 6 of its theatrical run. After a solid Day 5 that saw the film collect Rs 9.25 crore, an 8.8% jump over Day 4, the multi-starrer comedy has pushed its total India net past Rs 81 crore and its worldwide gross beyond Rs 120 crore. With Day 6 live tracking underway, the film remains firmly on course to hit its Rs 90-100 crore first-week domestic net target, even as it shares screens with the new Friday (July 3) release 'Alpha'.

title: Court orders new battery for faulty EV scooter, awards Rs 15K compensation
content: A district consumer commission has ordered Okinawa Scooters and its dealers to replace a defective electric scooter battery and speedometer free of charge. The directive comes after the company failed to honor the vehicle's warranty, leaving the owner with a non-functional scooter. The commission also awarded Rs 15,000 for compensation and litigation costs, highlighting the need for proper after-sales service for EV buyers.

title: Passport fees, commercial LPG prices, Aadhaar update, ITR deadline: What changes from today
content: Significant financial and policy shifts are now in effect across India. Households and businesses will see changes in fuel prices, rural employment guarantees, and credit card benefits. Aadhaar users can update emails for free, while banks face stricter consumer protection rules. Passport application fees have also increased, and EPFO online services are resuming after an upgrade.

title: 'I don't think he will...': Pujara on Sooryavanshi's India debut in England
content: Cheteshwar Pujara, the former Indian cricketer, offers sage advice to 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, indicating that he may not step onto the field during the upcoming England T20I series. Pujara encourages the young player to remain level-headed amidst the excitement, suggesting that he might find his opportunity only if the seasoned batsmen succeed.

title: TTE brings doctor to treat child’s fever on train for free
content: A heartwarming train journey from Maharashtra to Goa took an unexpected turn when a two-year-old developed a fever. Prompt action by a vendor and the TTE led to a doctor and assistant arriving at the next station, providing free medication and care. This experience highlighted for the traveler the significant positive changes and efficient services now prevalent in Indian Railways, sparking widespread appreciation online.

title: No wedding rituals, no valid Hindu marriage despite registration: Gujarat HC
content: A marriage certificate alone cannot validate a Hindu marriage if essential religious rites like Saptapadi were never performed, the Gujarat High Court has ruled. The court emphasized that registration merely proves an already valid marriage, not creates one. This decision nullified a marriage where the woman admitted no rituals took place, despite a registered certificate, highlighting the primacy of religious ceremonies over documentation.

title: Indian Techie, 33, Dies After Car Gets Swept Away In US Flash Floods
content: On Saturday afternoon 33-year-old Venkatesh Doppalapudi was driving to Houston when severe storms had already dropped more than 6 inches of rain in many parts of Kansas. During his journey his vehicle was washed away by the flash floods.

title: "Will Love You Unconditionally": Chinese Firm Sells Hyper-Real Robots
content: The company UBTech says its "U1" robot -- equipped with eye cameras, chest sensors, and listening microphones -- is the world's first full-sized, ultra-realistic humanoid designed for mass production.

title: Bulldozer Action Against Illegal Luxury Bungalows Near Statue Of Unity
content: BJP MLA Darshna Deshmukh Vasava said in 2022, he came to know about such incident through news reports.

title: Surat Civic Body Suspends 5 Over Slum Demolition It Claims It Did Not Order
content: According to Surat Municipal Corporation officials the reason for suspension is to ensure that the departmental investigation can be conducted in a completely fair, neutral, and unbiased manner

title: Lt General Rajesh Pushkar Takes Charge As Southern Command Chief
content: He succeeds Lieutenant General Sandeep Jain, who has taken over as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff, according to a defence release.

title: Unchecked AI Progress May Pose Catastrophic Risks, UN Panel Warns
content: The panel also outlined a range of safety concerns, such as the risk of losing control over AI systems as they become increasingly autonomous, and deceptive.

title: No Witnesses, How Cops Will Have To Pass 'Panchsheel' Test In Fort Murder
content: The prosecution will have to bank on circumstantial evidence in the murder of Ketan Agarwal, allegedly by Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary.

title: Siya Goyal, Lover's Snapchat Video Days Before Ketan Agarwal's Murder
content: Siya Goyal's parents have told the media she was not in a romantic relationship with Chetan Chaudhary, but were simply friends

title: AI Models By China, Japan May Have Led To US Lifting Mythos Ban
content: The Trump administration has lifted its export control ban on Anthropics two most powerful models - Mythos 5 and Fable 5.

title: Selfies At Murder Site: Inside $32.8 Billion Dark Tourism Industry
content: Academically, this phenomenon is called "Dark Tourism", a term coined by researchers John Lennon and Malcolm Foley to describe travel to sites associated with death, tragedy, and suffering.

title: GST At 9: Sectoral Hits And Misses, 9 Defining Legal Battles, What Remains
content: GST collections hit an all-time high of Rs 23.11 lakh crore in FY 25-26. April 2026 alone brought in a record Rs 2.42 lakh crore.

title: No More Red Lights? Delhi Plans Signal-Free Travel On 25 Busy Roads
content: Instead of building more flyovers, authorities are focusing on redesigning junctions to keep vehicles moving continuously.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 443 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Brent oil crude rose 0.5% to $73.32 while US WTI rose 0.59% to $69.91 per barrel.

title: UN Report Sees Enormous Potential Benefits And Big Risks From AI
content: AI adoption has accelerated broadly, but unevenly, across countries and sectors, said a report by a U.N. independent scientific panel on the technology.

title: Kerala Opposition Walks Out Of Assembly Over Low Alcohol Beverage Tax Bill
content: Responding to the allegations, Chief Minister V. D. Satheesan said the Bill had been cleared after discussions in the Business Advisory Committee, in which the Leader of the Opposition had also participated.

title: Glitch Caused False Hijacking Alarm On Tel Aviv Flight: Bulgarian Ministry
content: A transponder glitch as a Tel Aviv flight accidentally transmits Code 7500 a false hijack alarm, scrambling fighter jets before the aircraft safely diverted to Bulgaria.

title: 'Tree Collapse Beyond Human Control': Minister On Mumbai Schoolboy's Death
content: As Maharashtra Minister Sanjay Shirsat felt the heat of the questions, his responses appeared to be straying from his initial considered statements.

title: 32 Hours After Venezuela Earthquakes, 18-Month-Old Baby, Mother Rescued Alive
content: Dayana suffered injuries to both of her legs in the earthquake, but her baby escaped with only minor injuries.

title: Madras High Court sets aside appointment of 17 personal assistants to its judges
content: Justices S.M. Subramaniam and N. Senthilkumar find several “illegalities and irregularities” in the appointment process undertaken in 2023

title: Environmentalists allege bid to derail Muttil tree-felling case
content: Wayanad Prakruthi Samrakshana Samiti alleges that the Kerala government and the accused are acting in concert to sabotage the case.

title: All-party meeting opposes proposal to shift Anthiyur horse fair venue
content: The meeting resolved to submit a petition to Anthiyur Tahsildar and Block Development Officer on July 1, urging them not to shift the fair venue. Villagers to stage a road blockade if amicable solution is not reached.

title: Nearly 200 acres of forest land encroached in Telangana’s Vikarabad; eight acres recovered
content: Officials have woken up only recently to the rampant deforestation going on for several years, and are now busy in damage control exercise

title: Anthiyur horse fair venue dispute resolved after talks
content: During the meeting held at the temple, the temple administration assured horse owners that around 10 acres of land would be earmarked for temporary sheds and vehicle parking. Following the assurance, the association agreed to continue holding the fair at its existing venue, bringing to an end a week-long dispute.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Helicopter services suspended amid adverse weather conditions in Kedarnath Dham Yatra
content: District Magistrate of Rudraprayag, Vishal Mishra, said that all helicopter operations for the pilgrimage have been halted until further notice

title: T.N. Minister Nirmalkumar accuses DMK’s Senthilbalaji of attempting to lure TVK MLAs
content: Mr. Nirmalkumar claimed several TVK MLAs were approached by the DMK telling it intended to move a no-confidence motion against the Speaker in the next Assembly session and asked them to vote in support of it. One of the TVK MLAs lodged a complaint, based on which three persons were arrested.

title: Andhra Pradesh Information Commission dismisses 160 RTI cases, raps appellant over misuse
content: The Commission said Naidu Nagarjuna Reddy had filed thousands of applications and over 400 second appeals, using the RTI Act as a litigation tool rather than for transparency

title: Karnataka High Court declines to quash UAPA case against associates of US-based Christian missionary organisation The Timothy Initiative
content: One of the gravest threats to national security in the present times is, the clandestine funding of extremism, said Court

title: Kerala will not allow communalisation of education, no compromise on university autonomy: Roji M. John
content: Minister says Higher Education department will undertake a comprehensive review of the Four-Year Undergraduate Programme. Necessary reforms will be introduced to address the practical difficulties experienced by students and colleges

title: Kerala takes another step towards becoming ‘Keralam,’ Assembly unanimously approves President’s reference
content: House considers Kerala (Alteration of Name) Bill, 2026, which was referred by the President to seek its opinion. The legislators unanimously endorse all queries raised by the President on the Bill’s 10 clauses

title: MES demands SIR forms in Marathi, activist asks if Maharashtra is doing the same for Kannada-speaking people
content: During a door-to-door visit of SIR staff in Bandur Galli in  Belagavi on July 1, MES leader and former member of the Belagavi city corporation Sunil Balekundri refused to accept the enumeration forms in Kannada

title: Make learning and innovation lifelong pursuits, President Murmu tells Andhra graduates
content: At the first convocation of the Central University of Andhra Pradesh, the President said a degree should mark the beginning, not the end, of the pursuit of knowledge

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) MLC Sachin Ahir elected Maharashtra Legislative Council’s deputy Chairman as Mahayuti candidate
content: Opposition MVA fielded Jagannath Abhyankar of the Shiv Sena (UBT) for the post; however, on the appeal of Legislative Affairs Minister Chandrakant Patil, Shiv Sena (UBT) legislator Anil Parab said the Opposition was withdrawing Mr. Abhyankar’s candidature

title: Implementation of VB-G RAM-G without opposition amounts to failure in protecting Tamil Nadu’s financial well-being: Thangam Thennarasu
content: Under VB-G RAM-G scheme, which comes into force with effect from July 1, the State’s contribution has been increased to 40%. The Centre reducing its contribution for the Centrally-sponsored programmes will lead to fiscal strain on Tamil Nadu, he said.

title: Maharashtra minister privately admitted he didn't know TET exam papers printed in Agra, says BJP MLA
content: The State School Education Minister’s admission exposed serious lapses in the functioning of the School Education Department, the former Minister Sudhir Mungantiwar said and underscored the need for a comprehensive review of all State-level examinations

title: CPI(M), CPI leaders meet Tamil Nadu CM Vijay, press various demands
content: The TVK-led Tamil Nadu government, before tabling a resolution in the Assembly on Mekedatu issue, should have called an all-party meeting to take the issue forward, said CPI(M) leader P. Shanmugam

title: Kerala Assembly passes resolution urging Centre to withdraw amendments to FCRA rules
content: Resolution, moved by Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan, terms the amendments ‘a breach of Constitutional provisions and federal principles’

title: Why managing fiscal is a tightrope for State governments
content: Most State government expenditure is on social sectors, such as health and education, and economic sectors, such as agriculture and irrigation. In Kerala, such spending has driven social progress since the 1960s. The gap between expenditure and receipts is usually financed through market borrowings.

title: Foreign medical graduates allowed to take up CRMI in 36 non-teaching hospitals in Tamil Nadu
content: This has fulfilled a long-pending demand of hundreds of FMGs, who had passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination and were waiting for the mandatory CRMI

title: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit takes charge as new Vice-Chief of the Air Staff
content: Prior to the ceremony, the air officer visited the National War Memorial and laid a wreath to pay homage to the fallen heroes of the armed forces

title: Scientists use budget tech to probe long-standing physics mystery
content: The study was driven by a long-standing debate over whether ruthenium dioxide possesses a rare, unconventional type of magnetism called altermagnetism

title: Former staff of T.N. Civil Supplies Corporation complain to CM Vijay over ‘low’ off-take of wheat under PDS
content: The association suggested to Mr. Vijay that the State government seek higher allotment of wheat from the Centre in lieu of rice.

title: Ketan Agarwal murder: Fiancée kept victim’s phone with her for some time
content: According to a police official, the investigation has shown that Agarwal’s mobile phone came into Goyal’s possession after he was allegedly pushed off the cliff at the historical site

title: Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve core closed till September 30 for tiger breeding season
content: The core of the Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve is shut to tourists and pilgrims until September 30; its tiger count has risen from 47 in 2018 to 76 in 2024

title: Telangana’s OGH doctors perform 36-hour surgery to transplant five organs on one patient
content: All organs for the transplant were donated by a woman who was declared brain dead. The multi-visceral transplant is claimed as India’s first.

title: FM Sitharaman embarks on 4-day official visit to France to bolster economic ties
content: India and France will discuss potential areas for greater bilateral cooperation in various sectors, and further enhance the economic relations between India and France, an official statement said

title: First tranche of funds under Annapurna Yojana transferred to 1.1 crore women beneficiaries: Bengal CM
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that it is not possible to transfer taxpayers' money from government coffers to non-Indians, while justifying the rejection of 26 lakh Annapurna Yojana applications

title: Annapurna Yojana: Women beneficiaries to receive ₹3,000 in West Bengal’s new cash assistance scheme
content: Under the Annapura Yojana scheme, women will receive ₹3,000 per month. The existing Lakshmir Bhandar scheme will continue until the new programme is fully implemented

title: Rain disrupts Mumbai commuter services; waterlogging reported
content: The Central Railway and Western Railway officials issued statements regarding the rescheduling of some services. Passengers waited at stations for longer durations

title: Kerala Assembly: Minister says govt to prioritise battery energy storage systems to tackle power crisis
content: Five of six BESS projects on the anvil are expected to be completed by 2026-end and these will provide 1,500 MW in storage capability, Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph tells House

title: Sahitya Akademi award-winning poet Puviyarasu laid to rest with State honours in Coimbatore
content: Puviyarasu, a two-time Sahitya Akademi Award recipient and a founding member of the Vanampadi poetry movement, died on June 30, 2026

title: U.P. SIT probing Ram temple donation matter granted 15-day extension
content: The tenure of the Special Investigation Team (SIT) has been extended to probe the matter deeply and widen the scope of the probe

title: Private bus catches fire at Vadalur, passengers escape unhurt
content: The bus broke down when it reached Vadalur following which the bus crew evacuated the passengers. Within a few minutes, a fire broke out in the vehicle’s battery.

title: Madras High Court refuses to reject suit against continuance of Nadigar Sangam office-bearers after expiry of term
content: Justice A.D. Maria Clete dismisses an application filed by an association member to reject the suit file by another member

title: Thiruvananthapuram Corporation BJP councillor R. Sugathan remanded to police custody in temple festival assault case
content: After considering investigating team’s request to recover the weapon allegedly used in the attack, Nedumangad court allows police custody until 5.30 p.m. on Wednesday (July 1)

title: Bribery case: CBI arrests Haryana cadre IPS officer Deepak Gahlawat
content: The case stems from a CBI trap operation conducted in June, during which a Delhi Police Crime Branch Inspector and an alleged middleman were arrested

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay to hold consultative meeting with post-poll alliance partners on July 1
content: The meeting convened by the TVK assumes significance against the backdrop of the disintegration of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance following the 2026 Assembly poll debacle.

title: Influencer Joseph ‘Prasna Raavan’ held in Hyderabad for remarks against Pawan Kalyan
content: Joseph Bachalakuri, arrested in Hyderabad on a Jana Sena leader’s complaint, will be produced before a Pithapuram court; a case has been booked under the IT Act

title: Sri Sathya Sai Collector urges farmers to shift towards natural farming
content: Referring to the projected impact of the El Niño conditions, Shyam Prasad tells farmers not to leave their fields uncultivated, but to make optimum use of available soil moisture through timely sowing

title: Temple burglary gang behind 22 thefts across Karnataka nabbed; items worth ₹14.19 lakh seized
content: The police seized 25 gold thalis and other gold ornaments weighing 86 grams, three motorcycles, one autorickshaw, and two gas cylinders. The total value of the seized items is estimated at ₹14.19 lakh

title: Sahitya Akademi Award winner Puviyarasu no more
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay announced that the Tamil Nadu government would accord State honours at the poet’s funeral.

title: ‘Kafir screenshot’ case in Kerala: Sessions court to pronounce verdict on DYFI leader’s bail plea on July 3
content: Prosecution opposed the bail plea, claiming that Jithin Bhaskaran tried to destroy crucial digital evidence from his phone. It was also pointed out that his custodial interrogation was necessary to continue the probe

title: India adds 709 new species to its faunal database in 2025, 353 taxa to its flora
content: Kerala recorded the highest 98 new species of animals added to country’s database, while Arunachal Pradesh emerged as the leading contributor with 49 discoveries

title: SIR Enumeration Forms in English for GHMC, in Telugu for the rest; dummy forms printed in Urdu: Telangana CEO office
content: The EFs were distributed to nearly 60% of total 3.38 crore electors in the State, till 2 p.m. on June 30, 2026

title: Census 2027: Enumerators begin door-to-door visits for Houselisting phase in Kerala
content: More than 61,000 enumerators, mostly teachers and other government employees, have been deployed for the exercise. The State has been divided into houselisting blocks for data collection

title: Calcutta HC directs Bengal to frame guidelines for transporting accused persons
content: There has been a spate of egg attacks on accused persons, particularly local Trinamool leaders, councillors and former MLAs after their arrest since the change of regime in West Bengal

title: 50% traffic e-challan fine waiver nets ₹11.94 crore in 10 days for Bengaluru police
content: According to official data, a total of 3,97,506 traffic e-challan cases were disposed of through the prescribed modes of payment during the 10-day period

title: Tumakuru police crack crude bomb supply network after NH-48 blast
content: The investigation revealed that the explosives had allegedly been procured from George Niklov Fernandes (64) of Chandavar village in Honnavar taluk of Uttara Kannada district

title: Smaller LDF allies in Kerala mull broader platform, keep merger option open after Assembly poll rout
content: NCP faction led by A.K. Saseendran, Congress (Secular) and Indian Socialist Janata Dal exploring formation of a broader platform ahead of possible merger, while remaining part of Left coalition in Kerala

title: DMK seeks FIR over alleged participation of private individuals in Cabinet meetings
content: According to the complaint, John Arockiasamy and Vishnu Reddy, described as “close associates of the Chief Minister and residents of Andhra Pradesh”, had regularly attended Cabinet meetings, official review meetings and other high-level government deliberations held at the Secretariat.

title: Man gets life imprisonment for Chilakaluripet murder over extramarital dispute
content: The Narasaraopet court found Akkala Chenchayya guilty of killing Palaparthi Nagaraju in January 2025; proceedings against a juvenile co-accused are pending

title: Hubballi doctors carry out advanced surgery on man suffering severe breathing difficulty
content: Specialist surgeons at HCG Suchirayu Hospital, Hubballi, successfully performed an advanced surgery to treat a 48-year-old patient having a narrowed windpipe resulting in severe breathing difficulties

title: Shrimp farmers shift to local sales amid price drops, export disruptions
content: With exports affected by disruptions in international markets, shrimp farmers have turned to local sales, offering their produce at around ₹400 per kg

title: Assam duo launches digital bird repository with avian identification guide
content: Pokkhi not only seeks to showcase Assam’s avian diversity with the conservation status of each species but also to document their names in all languages spoken by the indigenous communities

title: Darkhast land and the Church of Our Lady of Assumption in Chennai
content: In Urdu, Darkhast means a petition or request, and in East India Company terms it came to mean, particularly in Madras Presidency, grant by the government of poromboke lands to homeless or downtrodden individuals.

title: Rs 35cr bribe to topple Vijay govt? TVK, DMK trade MLA poaching charges
content: the ruling TVK and opposition DMK accused each other of trying to poach their MLAs . televisionK linked the "attempt to topple TVK govt" to former minister V Senthil Balaji . the police arrested three people including Thirunavukkarasu, accusing them of attempting to induce televisionK .
